artPrompt: An ornate hero shot of a battered claw machine glowing hot-gold from within, its three-pronged steel claw dangling slack over a heap of grimy prizes and a single coiled skeleton, lurid neon reflecting off scratched glass. Tattered carnival tents and ember sparks haze the smoky black backdrop. Reverent low film-still angle isolating the rigged contraption, rendered as gritty cinematic digital concept art — highly detailed, dramatic photoreal lighting, atmospheric haze, realistic textures.
name: The Rigged Claw Machine
text: {2}, {T}: Choose a creature card in any graveyard. Roll a six-sided die. On a 5 or 6, the claw actually grips — return that card to the battlefield under your control. On a 1 through 4, the claw goes limp at the last second, as designed, and you create a Treasure as a consolation token.
flavorText: The claw's grip strength is calibrated by an accountant, not an engineer.
